name: AI Frontend Architect Rubric
audience: AI/frontend engineers repositioning into flagship proof-driven roles
weeklyTheme: Convert current frontend experience into visible AI proof.
headlineFocus: Angular and TypeScript interfaces for AI copilots

signals.label: Target role is explicit in public positioning
signals.gap: The profile headline does not clearly state the AI frontend target yet.
signals.nextAction: Rewrite the GitHub profile README and summary so AI frontend architecture is visible in the first screen.

signals.label: Enough public proof artifacts exist
signals.gap: There are not yet enough public proof artifacts for a recruiter to trust the pivot.
signals.nextAction: List at least three proof artifacts that show current work, not just planned direction.

signals.label: GitHub is part of the recruiter path
signals.gap: The profile lacks a GitHub link as a primary proof surface.
signals.nextAction: Link the GitHub profile and make the flagship repos recruiter-readable.

signals.label: Portfolio or public case-study surface exists
signals.gap: No portfolio or case-study surface is linked.
signals.nextAction: Add a portfolio link or public case-study page that explains what the projects prove.

signals.label: Proof mentions AI UI patterns recruiters expect
signals.gap: The current proof list does not yet show streaming, citations, or copilot UX clearly.
signals.nextAction: Publish or tighten one proof artifact around streaming chat, citation rendering, or grounded assistant UX.

signals.label: Tool execution and approval flows are visible
signals.gap: The profile does not yet demonstrate tool execution UX or human approval design.
signals.nextAction: Add one project or README section that shows tool-call visibility and approval handling.

signals.label: Core frontend depth is visible
signals.gap: The strong-skill section is too thin to support the repositioning story.
signals.nextAction: List the strongest frontend architecture skills that already support the transition.

signals.label: Interview-ready architecture narrative exists
signals.gap: There is not yet enough interview-safe narrative around real architecture decisions.
signals.nextAction: Write STAR stories and architecture notes for one real project and one proof project.

signals.label: Truthfulness constraints are explicit
signals.gap: The profile does not explicitly state truthfulness or mock-service constraints.
signals.nextAction: Add constraints that separate built proof from planned work and mocked integrations.

projectBlueprints.title: Angular AI Copilot Starter
projectBlueprints.problem: Most frontend portfolios do not prove streaming AI UX, cited responses, and safe action flows in one coherent artifact.
projectBlueprints.solution: Build a local-first Angular demo with streaming chat, RAG citation cards, tool timeline, and human approval states.
projectBlueprints.recruiterTakeaway: This project proves the candidate can design AI product interfaces beyond a basic chatbot shell.
interviewTalkingPoints: Partial token rendering without layout jank
How citations signal trust and confidence
How to represent tool execution visibly

projectBlueprints.title: UI-Aware Agent Demo
projectBlueprints.problem: Most AI demos show a chat panel, not an agent participating in the visible workflow.
projectBlueprints.solution: Build a demo with context inspector, suggested actions, approval checkpoints, and recovery states.
projectBlueprints.recruiterTakeaway: This proves the candidate understands agent-aware product UX and operational legibility.
interviewTalkingPoints: Difference between chat UI and UI-aware agents

projectBlueprints.title: Enterprise Agent Approval Workflow
projectBlueprints.problem: Enterprise AI tooling needs governance-grade action review, not only delight-driven UI.
projectBlueprints.solution: Build an approval queue, risk tiers, reviewer roles, and an audit log around simulated agent actions.
projectBlueprints.recruiterTakeaway: This proves enterprise judgment, not only frontend polish.
interviewTalkingPoints: What makes approval UX different from a confirm dialog
How to classify action risk tiers
How audit trails affect interface design
How to keep governance flows usable

readmeChecklist: Lead with what the project proves, not just the stack.
Show one screenshot, GIF, or concrete visual in the first screen.
Add a 'mocked vs real' section that is unambiguous.
Explain architecture decisions in recruiter-readable language.
Add a roadmap that separates built work from planned work.
Make local setup copy-paste simple and verified.
interviewTopics: Moving from mocks to real backends
truthfulnessGuardrails: Do not claim production usage unless the public artifact proves it.
Label mock services as mock services in every README and demo.
Separate built evidence from planned roadmap work.
Do not invent metrics, user counts, stars, or contribution outcomes.
Only publish claims you can defend in a recruiter screen or interview.
positioningComparison: Use occupation-ops when the main problem is weak public proof before applying.
Use job-search pipeline tools when the main problem is scanning, tracking, or operationalizing active applications.
Occupation-ops is not a tracker, scanner, or mass-apply workflow; it is a proof engine for candidate credibility.
